issues.title: requestAnimationFrame loop starts after component unmount via document.fonts.ready
issues.description: The useEffect declares `let animId: number` (uninitialized) and schedules work inside a `document.fonts.ready.then(...)` callback. If the component unmounts before fonts finish loading, the cleanup function runs with `animId = undefined`, so `cancelAnimationFrame(undefined)` is a no-op. When fonts eventually resolve, the Promise callback fires anyway — outside the React component lifecycle — and kicks off the recursive RAF draw loop with no way to cancel it. This loop calls `setState`-equivalent operations on an unmounted component and runs indefinitely.
issues.impact: Memory leak and potential 'Cannot update state on an unmounted component' errors in development. The animation loop continues consuming CPU after the component is gone.

issues.title: handleSearch swallows errors from searchUsers — unhandled promise rejection
issues.description: `handleSearch` uses `try/finally` without a `catch` block. If `searchUsers` (which propagates Supabase errors directly) throws — e.g., on a network failure or RLS policy error — the error propagates as an unhandled promise rejection. The `finally` block correctly resets `isSearching`, but `results` is never cleared, leaving stale results visible. There is no user-facing error message.
issues.impact: Unhandled promise rejection warnings/errors; stale search results remain on screen after a failed query; user has no feedback that the search failed.

issues.title: getInitials throws TypeError when email is an empty string
issues.description: When `name` is null/falsy, the function returns `email[0].toUpperCase()`. If `email` is an empty string `""`, `email[0]` evaluates to `undefined`, and calling `.toUpperCase()` on `undefined` throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'toUpperCase')`. This can occur if user data arrives with an empty email field.
issues.impact: Runtime crash (`TypeError`) wherever `getInitials` is called with an empty email string. Could crash user list/avatar rendering in the admin panel.

issues.title: Invalid total_cop silently defaults to 0, allowing $0 order creation
issues.description: When `total_cop` is not a valid finite number (e.g., `null`, `NaN`, `Infinity`, or missing entirely), the code silently substitutes `0` rather than returning a 400 error. This means a malformed or malicious request could create an order with a $0 total, bypassing payment validation.
issues.impact: Potential business logic bypass: orders with zero total could be processed. Downstream payment logic may assume a valid positive amount.
